input.description: Explore shadcn/ui component structure and patterns
input.prompt:
Explore the codebase to understand the structure and patterns used in shadcn/ui components. I need to understand:

1. The overall directory structure of the project
2. How existing compound components are built (look at Accordion, Tabs, or similar compound components with sub-components)
3. How React context is used within components
4. How components use "cn" utility for class merging
5. How TypeScript types and props are defined
6. How components handle orientation (horizontal/vertical)

Please look at:
- The root directory structure
- src/components/ui/ directory
- Any compound component like accordion.tsx, tabs.tsx, or navigation-menu.tsx

Return:
- The full directory tree of the project
- Full content of 2-3 compound components that show the best patterns (accordion, tabs, etc.)
- Any utility files like cn/utils
- Component file patterns (how files are structured, exported, typed)

Be thorough and return actual file contents.

input.description: Explore shadcn/ui registry system and demo examples
input.prompt:
Explore the registry system and demo examples in a shadcn/ui codebase. I need to understand:

1. Where and how components are registered (registry files)
2. Where demo/example files are stored and how they're structured
3. The format of registry entries for components and examples
4. How existing demos reference components

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with all the findings: ## Comprehensive Report: shadcn/ui Registry System and Demo Examples Based on my thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the registry system and demo/example structures: --- ### 1. REGISTRY FILES LOCATIONS **Main Registry Files:** - `/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx` - Auto-generated runtime registry index (1MB+, generated by build-registry.mts) - `/apps/v4/registry/__blocks__.json` - Blocks metadata - `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts` - Configuration and constants (design system schema) - `/apps/v4/registry/bases.ts` - Base configurations (Radix UI, Base UI) - `/apps/v4/registry/styles.tsx` - Available styles (Vega, Nova, Maia, Lyra, Mira, Luma) - `/apps/v4/registry/themes.ts` - Theme configurations - `/apps/v4/registry/base-colors.ts` - Base color definitions - `/apps/v4/registry/fonts.ts` - Font configurations - `/apps/v4/registry/directory.json` - Component directory (449KB, JSON metadata) **Base-Specific Registries:** - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts` - Base UI framework registry - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` - Radix UI framework registry --- ### 2. REGISTRY STRUCTURE: BASE AND RADIX DIRECTORIES Both bases (`base` and `radix`) follow identical organizational patterns: ``` registry/bases/{base-name}/ ├── ui/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for all UI components) │ ├── accordion.tsx │ ├── button.tsx │ ├── card.tsx │ ├── ... (58+ component files) ├── blocks/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for all blocks) │ ├── login-01/ │ │ ├── page.tsx │ │ └── components/ │ │ └── login-form.tsx │ ├── dashboard-01/ │ ├── sidebar-01 through sidebar-16/ │ ├── preview/ │ └── preview-02/ ├── examples/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for examples) │ └── [~60+ example files] ├── components/ ├── hooks/ ├── lib/ ├── internal/ └── registry.ts (Main registry file aggregating all) ``` **Key Directories:** - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/` - Base UI implementations - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/` - Radix UI implementations - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/` - 58 UI components - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/blocks/` - 16+ block templates (login, signup, dashboard, sidebar variations) --- ### 3. REGISTRY ENTRY FORMAT **Example from `_registry.ts` files:** ```typescript { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/.../accordion-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/accordion.md", }, }, } ``` **For blocks with dependencies:** ```typescript { name: "login-01", title: "Login 01", description: "A simple login form.", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: ["button", "card", "input", "label", "field"], files: [ { path: "blocks/login-01/page.tsx", target: "app/login/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", }, { path: "blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, ], categories: ["authentication", "login"], } ``` --- ### 4. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES **Location:** `/apps/v4/examples/{base}/*.tsx` and `/apps/v4/examples/base/*.tsx` **Auto-generated index:** `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` (416KB) **Example file structure:** Each example file is a standalone React component demonstrating a specific use case. Example names follow patterns: - `accordion-demo.tsx` - Basic/default demo - `accordion-basic.tsx` - Basic variant - `accordion-borders.tsx` - With borders variant - `accordion-card.tsx` - Card wrapper variant - `accordion-disabled.tsx` - Disabled state - `accordion-rtl.tsx` - Right-to-left support **Example count:** 435+ demo files per base (base and radix) --- ### 5. FULL CONTENT: KEY REGISTRY FILES ### **File 1: `/apps/v4/registry/bases.ts`** ```typescript import { type registryItemSchema } from "shadcn/schema" import { type z } from "zod" export const BASES: z.infer[] = [ { name: "radix", type: "registry:style", title: "Radix UI", description: "Optimized for fast development, easy maintenance, and accessibility.", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], meta: { logo: "", }, }, { name: "base", type: "registry:style", title: "Base UI", description: "Components for building accessible web apps and design systems.", dependencies: ["@base-ui/react"], meta: { logo: "", }, }, ] export type Base = (typeof BASES)[number] ``` ### **File 2: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` (excerpt)** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/accordion.md", }, }, }, { name: "alert", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/alert.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/alert", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx", }, }, }, { name: "alert-dialog", type: "registry:ui", registryDependencies: ["button"], files: [ { path: "ui/alert-dialog.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/alert-dialog", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-dialog-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/alert-dialog.md", }, }, }, // ... 58+ components total ] ``` ### **File 3: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/blocks/_registry.ts` (excerpt)** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const blocks: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "preview", title: "Preview", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: [ "alert-dialog", "avatar", "badge", "button", "button-group", "card", "checkbox", "combobox", "dropdown-menu", "empty", "field", "input", "input-group", "item", "label", "popover", "radio-group", "select", "separator", "sheet", "slider", "spinner", "switch", "textarea", "tooltip", "example", ], files: [ { path: "blocks/preview/index.tsx", type: "registry:block", }, ], }, { name: "login-01", title: "Login 01", description: "A simple login form.", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: ["button", "card", "input", "label", "field"], files: [ { path: "blocks/login-01/page.tsx", target: "app/login/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", }, { path: "blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, ], categories: ["authentication", "login"], }, // ... sidebar-01 through sidebar-16 ] ``` --- ### 6. FULL CONTENT: DEMO FILES ### **File 1: `/apps/v4/examples/base/accordion-demo.tsx`** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( What are your shipping options? We offer standard (5-7 days), express (2-3 days), and overnight shipping. Free shipping on international orders. What is your return policy? Returns accepted within 30 days. Items must be unused and in original packaging. Refunds processed within 5-7 business days. How can I contact customer support? Reach us via email, live chat, or phone. We respond within 24 hours during business days. ) } ``` ### **File 2: `/apps/v4/examples/base/accordion-basic.tsx`** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" const items = [ { value: "item-1", trigger: "How do I reset my password?", content: "Click on 'Forgot Password' on the login page, enter your email address, and we'll send you a link to reset your password. The link will expire in 24 hours.", }, { value: "item-2", trigger: "Can I change my subscription plan?", content: "Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time from your account settings. Changes will be reflected in your next billing cycle.", }, { value: "item-3", trigger: "What payment methods do you accept?", content: "We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and bank transfers. All payments are processed securely through our payment partners.", }, ] export function AccordionBasic() { return ( {items.map((item) => ( {item.trigger} {item.content} ))} ) } ``` ### **File 3: `/apps/v4/examples/base/button-demo.tsx`** ```typescript import { ArrowUpIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button" export default function ButtonDemo() { return (
) } ``` ### **File 4: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx`** ```typescript import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils" import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/card" import { Field, FieldDescription, FieldGroup, FieldLabel, } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/field" import { Input } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/input" export function LoginForm({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return (
Login to your account Enter your email below to login to your account
Email
Password Forgot your password?
Don't have an account? Sign up
) } ``` --- ### 7. AUTO-GENERATED INDEXES **`/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx` (excerpt):** Lazy-loads components dynamically: ```typescript export const Index: Record> = { "new-york-v4": { accordion: { name: "accordion", title: "undefined", description: "", type: "registry:ui", registryDependencies: undefined, files: [ { path: "registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", target: "", }, ], component: React.lazy(async () => { const mod = await import("@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion") const exportName = Object.keys(mod).find( (key) => typeof mod[key] === "function" || typeof mod[key] === "object" ) || "accordion" return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] } }), }, // ... more components }, } ``` **`/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` (excerpt):** ```typescript export const ExamplesIndex: Record> = { radix: { "accordion-basic": { name: "accordion-basic", filePath: "examples/radix/accordion-basic.tsx", component: React.lazy(async () => { const mod = await import("./radix/accordion-basic") const exportName = Object.keys(mod).find( (key) => typeof mod[key] === "function" || typeof mod[key] === "object" ) || "accordion-basic" return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] } }), }, // ... more examples }, } ``` --- ### 8. REGISTRY BUILD PIPELINE **Script location:** `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` **Build process:** 1. Builds `registry/bases/__index__.tsx` from authored base registries 2. Generates styled registries under `registry/{base-style}/` 3. Builds `registry/__index__.tsx` for runtime lookup 4. Builds `examples/__index__.tsx` from authored demos 5. Exports `public/r/*` through shadcn CLI 6. Copies compiled UI components to `styles/{style}/ui/` 7. Generates RTL variants for base-nova and radix-nova 8. Formats all generated outputs 9. Cleans temporary files --- ### 9. COMPONENT IMPORT PATHS Components are imported from style-specific locations: ```typescript // From demo files - style specific import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button" import { Accordion } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" // From blocks - base specific import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/button" import { Card } from "@/registry/bases/radix/ui/card" ``` --- ### 10. CONFIGURATION FILES - `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts` - Design system configuration with Zod schemas - `/apps/v4/registry/styles.tsx` - Style definitions (name, title, description, icon) - `/apps/v4/registry/fonts.ts` - Font configuration - `/apps/v4/registry/themes.ts` - Theme specifications - `/apps/v4/package.json` - Dependencies and scripts --- ### SUMMARY The shadcn/ui registry system is a sophisticated, multi-layered architecture: 1. **Two framework bases** (Radix UI, Base UI) each with independent UI component sets 2. **Six visual styles** (Vega, Nova, Maia, Lyra, Mira, Luma) that can be combined with bases 3. **Auto-generated indexes** that create lazy-loaded component references 4. **Block templates** for complex UIs (login forms, dashboards, sidebars) 5. **600+ demo examples** showing different variations and use cases 6. **TypeScript registry schema** validating all entries 7. **Build pipeline** that transforms source components into style-specific implementations The entire system is managed through the `build-registry.mts` script which automatically generates index files from source registries.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with all the findings: ## Comprehensive Report: shadcn/ui Registry System and Demo Examples Based on my thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the registry system and demo/example structures: --- ### 1. REGISTRY FILES LOCATIONS **Main Registry Files:** - `/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx` - Auto-generated runtime registry index (1MB+, generated by build-registry.mts) - `/apps/v4/registry/__blocks__.json` - Blocks metadata - `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts` - Configuration and constants (design system schema) - `/apps/v4/registry/bases.ts` - Base configurations (Radix UI, Base UI) - `/apps/v4/registry/styles.tsx` - Available styles (Vega, Nova, Maia, Lyra, Mira, Luma) - `/apps/v4/registry/themes.ts` - Theme configurations - `/apps/v4/registry/base-colors.ts` - Base color definitions - `/apps/v4/registry/fonts.ts` - Font configurations - `/apps/v4/registry/directory.json` - Component directory (449KB, JSON metadata) **Base-Specific Registries:** - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts` - Base UI framework registry - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` - Radix UI framework registry --- ### 2. REGISTRY STRUCTURE: BASE AND RADIX DIRECTORIES Both bases (`base` and `radix`) follow identical organizational patterns: ``` registry/bases/{base-name}/ ├── ui/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for all UI components) │ ├── accordion.tsx │ ├── button.tsx │ ├── card.tsx │ ├── ... (58+ component files) ├── blocks/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for all blocks) │ ├── login-01/ │ │ ├── page.tsx │ │ └── components/ │ │ └── login-form.tsx │ ├── dashboard-01/ │ ├── sidebar-01 through sidebar-16/ │ ├── preview/ │ └── preview-02/ ├── examples/ │ ├── _registry.ts (Registry definitions for examples) │ └── [~60+ example files] ├── components/ ├── hooks/ ├── lib/ ├── internal/ └── registry.ts (Main registry file aggregating all) ``` **Key Directories:** - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/` - Base UI implementations - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/` - Radix UI implementations - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/` - 58 UI components - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/blocks/` - 16+ block templates (login, signup, dashboard, sidebar variations) --- ### 3. REGISTRY ENTRY FORMAT **Example from `_registry.ts` files:** ```typescript { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/.../accordion-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/accordion.md", }, }, } ``` **For blocks with dependencies:** ```typescript { name: "login-01", title: "Login 01", description: "A simple login form.", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: ["button", "card", "input", "label", "field"], files: [ { path: "blocks/login-01/page.tsx", target: "app/login/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", }, { path: "blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, ], categories: ["authentication", "login"], } ``` --- ### 4. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES **Location:** `/apps/v4/examples/{base}/*.tsx` and `/apps/v4/examples/base/*.tsx` **Auto-generated index:** `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` (416KB) **Example file structure:** Each example file is a standalone React component demonstrating a specific use case. Example names follow patterns: - `accordion-demo.tsx` - Basic/default demo - `accordion-basic.tsx` - Basic variant - `accordion-borders.tsx` - With borders variant - `accordion-card.tsx` - Card wrapper variant - `accordion-disabled.tsx` - Disabled state - `accordion-rtl.tsx` - Right-to-left support **Example count:** 435+ demo files per base (base and radix) --- ### 5. FULL CONTENT: KEY REGISTRY FILES ### **File 1: `/apps/v4/registry/bases.ts`** ```typescript import { type registryItemSchema } from "shadcn/schema" import { type z } from "zod" export const BASES: z.infer[] = [ { name: "radix", type: "registry:style", title: "Radix UI", description: "Optimized for fast development, easy maintenance, and accessibility.", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], meta: { logo: "", }, }, { name: "base", type: "registry:style", title: "Base UI", description: "Components for building accessible web apps and design systems.", dependencies: ["@base-ui/react"], meta: { logo: "", }, }, ] export type Base = (typeof BASES)[number] ``` ### **File 2: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` (excerpt)** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/accordion.md", }, }, }, { name: "alert", type: "registry:ui", files: [ { path: "ui/alert.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/alert", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx", }, }, }, { name: "alert-dialog", type: "registry:ui", registryDependencies: ["button"], files: [ { path: "ui/alert-dialog.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/alert-dialog", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-dialog-example.tsx", api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/alert-dialog.md", }, }, }, // ... 58+ components total ] ``` ### **File 3: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/blocks/_registry.ts` (excerpt)** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const blocks: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "preview", title: "Preview", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: [ "alert-dialog", "avatar", "badge", "button", "button-group", "card", "checkbox", "combobox", "dropdown-menu", "empty", "field", "input", "input-group", "item", "label", "popover", "radio-group", "select", "separator", "sheet", "slider", "spinner", "switch", "textarea", "tooltip", "example", ], files: [ { path: "blocks/preview/index.tsx", type: "registry:block", }, ], }, { name: "login-01", title: "Login 01", description: "A simple login form.", type: "registry:block", registryDependencies: ["button", "card", "input", "label", "field"], files: [ { path: "blocks/login-01/page.tsx", target: "app/login/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", }, { path: "blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, ], categories: ["authentication", "login"], }, // ... sidebar-01 through sidebar-16 ] ``` --- ### 6. FULL CONTENT: DEMO FILES ### **File 1: `/apps/v4/examples/base/accordion-demo.tsx`** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( What are your shipping options? We offer standard (5-7 days), express (2-3 days), and overnight shipping. Free shipping on international orders. What is your return policy? Returns accepted within 30 days. Items must be unused and in original packaging. Refunds processed within 5-7 business days. How can I contact customer support? Reach us via email, live chat, or phone. We respond within 24 hours during business days. ) } ``` ### **File 2: `/apps/v4/examples/base/accordion-basic.tsx`** ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" const items = [ { value: "item-1", trigger: "How do I reset my password?", content: "Click on 'Forgot Password' on the login page, enter your email address, and we'll send you a link to reset your password. The link will expire in 24 hours.", }, { value: "item-2", trigger: "Can I change my subscription plan?", content: "Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time from your account settings. Changes will be reflected in your next billing cycle.", }, { value: "item-3", trigger: "What payment methods do you accept?", content: "We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and bank transfers. All payments are processed securely through our payment partners.", }, ] export function AccordionBasic() { return ( {items.map((item) => ( {item.trigger} {item.content} ))} ) } ``` ### **File 3: `/apps/v4/examples/base/button-demo.tsx`** ```typescript import { ArrowUpIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button" export default function ButtonDemo() { return (

AUTO-GENERATED INDEXES **`/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx` (excerpt):** Lazy-loads components dynamically: ```typescript export const Index: Record> = { "new-york-v4": { accordion: { name: "accordion", title: "undefined", description: "", type: "registry:ui", registryDependencies: undefined, files: [ { path: "registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", target: "", }, ], component: React.lazy(async () => { const mod = await import("@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion") const exportName = Object.keys(mod).find( (key) => typeof mod[key] === "function" || typeof mod[key] === "object" ) || "accordion" return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] } }), }, // ... more components }, } ``` **`/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` (excerpt):** ```typescript export const ExamplesIndex: Record> = { radix: { "accordion-basic": { name: "accordion-basic", filePath: "examples/radix/accordion-basic.tsx", component: React.lazy(async () => { const mod = await import("./radix/accordion-basic") const exportName = Object.keys(mod).find( (key) => typeof mod[key] === "function" || typeof mod[key] === "object" ) || "accordion-basic" return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] } }), }, // ... more examples }, } ``` --- ### 8. Perfect! Now let me compile all the information and provide you with a comprehensive summary of the registry format and patterns.

## Summary: shadcn/ui Registry Format and Patterns

Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's the exact registry format, types, and patterns:

---

### 1. **Registry Item TypeScript Interface**

From the files, registry items follow this structure (from `shadcn/schema`):

```typescript
{
  name: string                          // kebab-case identifier
  type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example" | "registry:style" | ...
  title?: string                        // Human-readable title (examples)
  files: Array<{
    path: string                        // Relative path to file
    type: string                        // File type
  }>
  registryDependencies?: string[]       // Array of registry item names this depends on
  dependencies?: string[]               // NPM package dependencies (e.g., "radix-ui")
  devDependencies?: string[]
  meta?: {
    links?: {
      docs?: string
      examples?: string
      api?: string
    }
  }
  css?: object                          // CSS definitions
  cssVars?: object
}
```

---

### 2. **Key Differences: Radix vs New York v4**

#### **Radix Registry** (`/bases/radix/`):
- **UI Registry** (`ui/_registry.ts`): Includes `meta.links` with documentation URLs
- **Examples Registry** (`examples/_registry.ts`): 
  - Uses naming pattern: `{component}-example` (e.g., `accordion-example`)
  - Includes `title` field for display
  - Has `registryDependencies` listing ALL dependencies (component + helpers like "example", "badge", "button", "card")
  - Example: `accordion-example` depends on `["accordion", "button", "card", "example"]`

#### **New York v4 Registry** (`/new-york-v4/`):
- **UI Registry** (`ui/_registry.ts`): 
  - Includes `dependencies: ["radix-ui"]` field in addition to `registryDependencies`
  - NO `meta.links` in v4 (cleaner)
  - Same `registryDependencies` pattern for components that depend on others (e.g., `alert-dialog` depends on `["button"]`)
- **Examples Registry** (`examples/_registry.ts`):
  - Uses naming pattern: `{component}-demo` (e.g., `accordion-demo`) - NOT `-example`
  - NO `title` field
  - `registryDependencies` only lists the component being demonstrated (e.g., `accordion-demo` depends on `["accordion"]`)
  - Minimal compared to radix

---

### 3. **Example Pattern Details**

**Radix accordion-example** (Full Complexity):
- File: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx`
- Registry entry: `name: "accordion-example"`, `registryDependencies: ["accordion", "button", "card", "example"]`
- Actually imports multiple components: accordion, button, card, example wrapper
- Uses `ExampleWrapper` and `Example` components for structure
- Contains multiple sub-examples (AccordionBasic, AccordionMultiple, etc.)

**New York v4 accordion-demo** (Minimal):
- File: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/accordion-demo.tsx` (would exist)
- Registry entry: `name: "accordion-demo"`, `registryDependencies: ["accordion"]`
- Only depends on the component being demonstrated

---

### 4. **File Locations & Directory Structure**

```
/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/
  ├── registry.ts              # Main registry export combining all
  ├── ui/                       # Component definitions
  │   └── _registry.ts         # Registry entries for UI components
  ├── examples/                 # Demo files
  │   └── _registry.ts         # Registry entries for examples
  ├── blocks/                   # Block components
  ├── charts/                   # Chart components
  ├── hooks/
  ├── lib/
  └── internal/
```

---

### 5. **Registry Composition** (from `registry.ts`)

The main registry combines entries:
```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    { name: "index", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    { name: "style", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    ...ui,
    ...examples,
    ...lib,
    ...components,
    ...internal,
    ...blocks,
    ...hooks,
    ...fonts,
  ]),
}
```

---

### 6. **UI Component Example: tabs.tsx**

Full component (91 lines):
- Uses Radix UI `Tabs` primitive as `TabsPrimitive`
- Exports: `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`, `tabsListVariants`
- Implements CVA variants (default, line)
- Uses data attributes for state (`data-slot`, `data-variant`, `data-active`)
- Fully styled with Tailwind with dark mode support

---

### 7. **Base Registry Entry Structure**

Both `/bases/base/` and `/bases/radix/` follow the same structure but with different primitive sources:
- Base uses `base-ui` primitives
- Radix uses `radix-ui` primitives
- Both have identical registry structure and example patterns

---

### Key Takeaways for Implementation:

1. **Components**: `type: "registry:ui"`, optional `registryDependencies` for component dependencies
2. **Examples (Radix)**: `type: "registry:example"`, name ends in `-example`, includes `title`, `registryDependencies` lists all imports
3. **Examples (New York v4)**: `type: "registry:example"`, name ends in `-demo`, no `title`, minimal `registryDependencies`
4. **Meta links**: Radix includes them, v4 doesn't
5. **Dependencies vs registryDependencies**: 
   - `dependencies`: NPM packages
   - `registryDependencies`: Other registry items to install

All file paths are absolute as specified above.

content.thinking:
Now I have a very clear and complete picture of the codebase. Let me now design the implementation plan and write it to the plan file.

## Summary of what I know:

### Architecture
- The project has two registries:
  1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/`** — the primary Next.js app registry
  2. **`apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/`** — Radix-specific base registry
  3. **`apps/v4/registry/bases/base/`** — Base UI base registry

### File locations
- **UI components** go in:
  - `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`
  - `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`
- **Demo examples** go in:
  - `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`
  - `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

### Registry entries go in:
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` — add stepper entry
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` — add stepper demo entry
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` — add stepper entry
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` — add stepper-example entry

The user explicitly says to add registry entries, so modifying the `_registry.ts` files is allowed—I just need to avoid changing existing component implementations. Now I'm thinking through what the Stepper component actually needs to support.

The component will have a root `Stepper` that manages state through React context, with individual `StepperItem` wrappers for each step. I'll need clickable triggers to navigate between steps, along with title and description elements, separators between steps, and content panels that display when a step is active. A custom hook will let child components access the context. The state will track the current step index, total number of steps, orientation (horizontal or vertical), and a callback for when the step changes.

For the controlled/uncontrolled pattern, I'll support both a `defaultStep` prop for uncontrolled mode and a `step` prop for controlled mode. Horizontal orientation arranges steps in a row with horizontal separators, while vertical stacks them in a column with vertical lines between them. Each step can be in one of three states: active (current), completed (before current), or pending (after current).

I'm realizing I need two separate contexts here — one for the overall stepper that holds the current step, orientation, and navigation logic, and another for individual step items that tracks their index and status. This way each `StepperItem` can inject its own context so its child components know which step they belong to.

The stepper will be a custom React component without any Radix UI dependency, since there's no existing headless library for this pattern. For the `new-york-v4` registry entry, I won't need to add `radix-ui` as a dependency unless I'm using something like the Slot/asChild pattern. The demo will be minimal for the `new-york-v4` version with a default export, while the Radix version can be more comprehensive.

Now I'm mapping out the file structure — I need to create the stepper component and demo files for both the `new-york-v4` and `radix` registries, then update the corresponding registry entries to include these new components.

Setting up the component implementation...

I'm defining the custom hooks to access the stepper and stepper item contexts, with error handling if they're used outside their proper providers. Then I'm building out the main Stepper component that manages the current step state, handles step navigation, and provides context to its children. Writing the Stepper component... Writing component styles... Finishing up the component definitions...

Now I'm noticing a potential issue with how `totalSteps` is being calculated—it's counting all children elements rather than filtering for just `StepperItem` components, which could throw off the step count if there are other child elements mixed in.

For the separator positioning in horizontal mode, I need to think through the layout more carefully. The separator should visually connect the trigger circles horizontally, sitting between each step's trigger and the next one. The typical structure alternates between `StepperItem` and `StepperSeparator` components, with titles and descriptions nested inside the items. I'm realizing the separator positioning is the real challenge here—in horizontal mode, it needs to sit between the trigger circles at the same vertical level, while in vertical mode it connects them vertically. I could either position the separator absolutely or use data attributes to cascade different styles based on orientation, but I need to think through how the Separator component actually integrates into this structure.

Building the separator component...

For the horizontal stepper layout, I'm considering the most straightforward approach where the triggers and separators align naturally in the flow.

For vertical steppers, the separator should sit inside each step item below the trigger, which changes how the components nest together. Let me think through how other libraries handle this pattern.

Actually, keeping the separator as an explicit sibling of StepperItem gives me more flexibility—the separator can just read the orientation from context and style itself accordingly. For horizontal layouts, I'm using flexbox with the separator taking up available space and positioned at the trigger's vertical center. For vertical layouts, the separator becomes a thin vertical line that stretches down, aligned with the trigger's horizontal center.

The key insight is that `mt-4` (half the trigger's 32px height) perfectly centers the separator on the trigger circle in horizontal mode. Now I'm wrestling with where `StepperContent` should live—nesting it inside `StepperItem` keeps the structure simple and works well for vertical steppers, though horizontal steppers would need content positioned below the entire row instead.

Looking at how similar components like Tabs handle this, I think the most flexible approach is letting `StepperContent` work both ways: nested inside `StepperItem` for vertical layouts, or as a sibling outside for horizontal ones. The challenge is that content placed outside loses access to the context, so I'll add a `step` prop to `StepperContent` to let it identify which step it belongs to when used independently.

Adding step prop to StepperContent...

Now I'm realizing `totalSteps` is tricky since counting children won't work—there are separators mixed in. I should probably make it an explicit prop on `Stepper` instead of trying to compute it automatically. I'm realizing that `React.Children.count` would count separators too, giving us 5 instead of 3, so that won't work. The cleanest solution is to just make `totalSteps` a required prop—users pass it explicitly and we use it for bounds checking in `goToStep`.

Actually, even simpler: skip the bounds checking entirely and let `goToStep` just fire the callback without validation. The step numbering becomes completely user-defined, which is more flexible and aligns with shadcn/ui's philosophy of keeping things minimal.

Now I need to think through how `StepperSeparator` gets the orientation from context.

Since `StepperSeparator` uses `useStepper()`, it has to be rendered inside the Stepper component, which is fine. For `StepperTrigger`, I should make it a button element for keyboard accessibility. I'm also considering accessibility attributes: the stepper indicator section should have `role="list"`, each item `role="listitem"`, the active step gets `aria-current="step"`, and the trigger needs an appropriate aria-label.

For the registry entries, the instructions say I can modify the `_registry.ts` files to add new component entries—I just can't touch existing component files.

Now I'm mapping out the full implementation: I'll create the main stepper component and a demo for the new-york-v4 registry, plus a radix base version with its own example. Then I'll add the necessary registry entries to both the UI and examples registries.

# Stepper Compound Component — Implementation Plan ## Context shadcn/ui needs a `Stepper` compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. No Radix UI primitive exists for steppers, so this is a fully custom component built with React context (like `Sidebar`) rather than wrapping a headless primitive (like `Accordion`). The component must support both horizontal and vertical orientations, follow all shadcn/ui patterns (`data-slot`, `cn()`, CVA where appropriate, `"use client"`), and be registered in all three registries: `new-york-v4`, `bases/radix`, and `bases/base`. --- ## Architecture Overview ### Sub-components | Component | Element | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `Stepper` | `
` | Root container; provides `StepperContext` | | `StepperList` | `
    ` | Wraps all items; computes step order & completion | | `StepperItem` | `

**6 registry edits** (append-only, no modifications to existing component code):
- `new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` — add `stepper` entry
- `new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` — add `stepper-demo` entry
- `bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` — add `stepper` entry (with `meta.links`)
- `bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` — add `stepper-example` entry
- `bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` — add `stepper` entry
- `bases/base/examples/_registry.ts` — add `stepper-example` entry

## Component Design

**9 sub-components:** `Stepper`, `StepperList`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperPanel`

**2 React contexts:**
- `StepperContext` (root) — shares `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`
- `StepperItemContext` (per-item, set by `StepperList`) — shares `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isDisabled`, `index`

**Key design choices:**
- `StepperList` uses `React.Children.toArray()` to read child `value` props in order, auto-computes `isCompleted` as `index < activeIndex`, then wraps each child in `StepperItemContext.Provider`
- `StepperItem` destructures `value`/`completed`/`disabled` props so they don't leak to the DOM
- `StepperPanel` uses `hidden={!isActive}` (not unmounting) to preserve form state
- `StepperIndicator` shows `<CheckIcon>` when completed, step number otherwise
- Orientation drives layout via `data-orientation` + Tailwind classes
- All patterns follow shadcn/ui conventions: `"use client"`, `data-slot`, `cn()`, controlled/uncontrolled state, focus-visible ring styles

- `StepperItemContext` (per-item, set by `StepperList`) — `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isDisabled`, `index`

## Implementation Complete

### 6 new files created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|

| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` | Base UI version (`@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils`) |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Base UI multi-variant example |

### 6 registry entries added (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`)

- `new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` → `stepper` UI entry (line 565)
- `new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` → `stepper-demo` example entry (line 2126)
- `bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` → `stepper` UI entry with `meta.links` (line 833)

### Component architecture

**9 sub-components:** `Stepper` · `StepperList` · `StepperItem` · `StepperTrigger` · `StepperIndicator` · `StepperTitle` · `StepperDescription` · `StepperSeparator` · `StepperPanel`

**2 React contexts:** `StepperContext` (root — `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`) and `StepperItemContext` (per-item — `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isDisabled`, `index`)

**Key behaviours:**
- `StepperList` reads children's `value` props to auto-compute `isCompleted = index < activeIndex`, then wraps each `StepperItem` in `StepperItemContext.Provider`
- Both **controlled** (`value` + `onValueChange`) and **uncontrolled** (`defaultValue`) patterns supported
- `StepperIndicator` renders a `<CheckIcon>` when completed, step number otherwise
- `StepperPanel` uses `hidden={!isActive}` to keep DOM mounted (preserves form state)
- `StepperSeparator` turns from `bg-border` to `bg-primary` when the step is completed
- `orientation="horizontal"` (default) or `"vertical"` — layout changes via Tailwind conditional classes driven by the context value
- `useStepper()` hook exported for advanced use cases
- `data-slot`, `data-active`, `data-completed`, `data-disabled`, `data-orientation` on all elements for CSS targeting
